tagline_en: After this module, 'a 100MW campus', 'PUE 1.1', '800VDC' and 'behind-the-meter gas' stop being buzzwords — you can trace every watt from the grid substation to the sub-1V pin of a GPU, and explain why electrons, not chips, became AI's scarcest input.
tagline_zh: 读完本讲,「100MW 园区」「PUE 1.1」「800VDC」「表后发电」不再是黑话——你能把每一瓦电从电网变电站一路追到 GPU 上不足 1 伏的引脚,并说清为什么 AI 最稀缺的原料从芯片变成了电子。

sections.heading_zh: 一栋用兆瓦计量的建筑
sections.body_en:
Drive past a datacenter and you see a windowless warehouse. But the industry never describes one by floor area — it describes it by power draw, in megawatts (MW), because electricity is the input every other subsystem scales from. Every watt fed into a chip comes back out as heat that must be removed, so power sets the size of the cooling plant. Power determines the transformers, the backup generators, the batteries. And for an AI cloud, power is literally the unit of revenue: SemiAnalysis estimates an AI cloud earns roughly $10-12M of revenue per megawatt per year. The most useful mental model for this whole module: a datacenter is a single machine whose job is converting electrons into tokens.

Two pieces of jargon carve up the building. White space is where the computers live — the halls of racks. Grey space is everything that feeds them: transformer rooms, switchgear, walls of UPS batteries, plus the yard outside — a dedicated substation, diesel generators, chillers and cooling towers. In an AI datacenter, grey space and the yard are most of the capital cost.

Now make '100MW' concrete. Nvidia's flagship GB200 NVL72 rack draws about 120kW and packs 72 GPUs. So 100MW of IT load is roughly 830 such racks — on the order of 60,000 GPUs — spread across a few halls, fed by their own substation, earning around a billion dollars of AI cloud revenue a year. And 100MW is no longer 'big': xAI's Colossus 2 crossed the gigawatt (1,000MW) line, and multi-GW campuses are in planning. At this scale, a datacenter is not a building with some electrical gear inside; it is a power plant's worth of load with a building wrapped around it.
sections.body_zh:
开车路过一座数据中心,你看到的是一栋没有窗户的大仓库。但这个行业从不用建筑面积描述它,而是用功率——兆瓦(MW)。原因在于:电力是所有其他子系统的共同标尺。喂进芯片的每一瓦电,最终都会变成必须排走的热,所以功率决定散热系统的规模;功率决定变压器、备用发电机、电池的大小;而对 AI 云来说,功率干脆就是收入的计量单位——SemiAnalysis 估算,AI 云每兆瓦每年营收约 1,000-1,200 万美元。本讲最有用的心智模型一句话:数据中心是一台把电子转换成 token 的机器。

两个行话先把这栋楼切开。白空间(white space)是计算机住的地方——一排排机柜所在的机房大厅;灰空间(grey space)是喂养它们的一切:变压器房、开关柜、成墙的 UPS 电池,再加上楼外的院子——专用变电站、柴油发电机、冷机和冷却塔。在 AI 数据中心里,灰空间加院子占了资本开支的大头。

把「100MW」翻译成实物:英伟达旗舰机柜 GB200 NVL72 功耗约 120kW、装 72 颗 GPU。100MW 的 IT 负载,大约就是 830 个这样的机柜——约 6 万颗 GPU——分布在几个机房大厅里,由自己的变电站供电,每年挣约 10 亿美元的 AI 云收入。而 100MW 早已算不上「大」:xAI 的 Colossus 2 已跨过吉瓦(1,000MW)门槛,规划中的园区动辄数吉瓦。到了这个体量,数据中心已经不是「装了些电气设备的建筑」,而是一座电厂级的负荷,外面裹了一层房子。

sections.heading_en: The Power Chain: From 345,000 Volts to Less Than One
sections.heading_zh: 电力链:从 34.5 万伏到不足 1 伏
sections.body_en:
Electricity arrives at the site at transmission voltage — commonly 115,000 to 345,000 volts — and ends its journey at under 1 volt at the GPU's pins. The whole electrical design of a datacenter is a staircase down that range, governed by one physics rule: for a given power, current falls as voltage rises (power = voltage × current), and the heat wasted in a wire grows with the square of the current (I²R). Voltage is water pressure: high pressure lets a thin pipe carry the same flow; low pressure demands an enormous pipe — in electrical terms, absurd amounts of copper.

The staircase: the onsite substation steps transmission down to medium voltage (13.8-45kV), which runs to each building. Transformers step that to low voltage (~415V AC). Switchgear — industrial circuit breakers — protects each branch. The UPS (uninterruptible power supply), a wall of batteries, rides through flickers and carries the whole load for the seconds-to-minutes the diesel generators need to start. Overhead busways — rigid copper rails, a highway for current — run above the rack rows. Inside the rack, power shelves rectify AC to 54V DC onto a copper busbar running down the rack's spine, and finally VRMs (voltage regulator modules) sitting millimeters from each GPU make the last step to below 1V.

AI broke the bottom of this staircase. At 48-54V, a 600kW-class rack would draw ~12,500 amps — a megawatt-class rack would need ~200kg of copper busbar, with power shelves crowding out the compute. At 800V DC the same rack draws ~750A: ~16.7x less current, and roughly 220-278x less resistive heating. Hence the industry-wide move to 800VDC (Nvidia's reference design; Google/Meta/Microsoft's ±400V variant reuses the electric-vehicle supply chain): rectification moves out of the rack into a dedicated 'power rack' sidecar (~$400-500k apiece), the central UPS is eventually eliminated, and end-to-end electrical efficiency rises from ~82% toward ~87.4% — worth ~50MW of freed power at a gigawatt campus. The endgame is the solid-state transformer: one semiconductor box converting medium voltage straight to 800VDC.
sections.body_zh:
电到达园区时是输电电压——通常 11.5 万到 34.5 万伏;旅程的终点是 GPU 引脚上的不足 1 伏。数据中心的整个电气设计,就是在这个区间里往下走的一段楼梯,背后只有一条物理规则:功率一定时,电压越高电流越小(功率 = 电压 × 电流),而导线上浪费的热随电流的平方增长(I²R)。电压就是水压:压力高,细管子也能送同样的流量;压力低,就得用粗得离谱的管子——换成电的语言,就是天量的铜。

这段楼梯依次是:园区变电站把输电电压降到中压(13.8-45kV),送到每栋楼;变压器再降到低压(约 415V 交流);开关柜——工业级断路器——保护每条支路;UPS(不间断电源),一面电池墙,负责扛过电压闪变,并在柴油发电机启动前的几秒到几分钟里独自撑起全部负载;母线槽——架在机柜列上方的刚性铜轨,电流的高速公路——把电送到每一列;进了机柜,电源架把交流整流成 54V 直流,灌进沿机柜脊柱走的铜母排;最后,贴在每颗 GPU 毫米之遥的 VRM(电压调节模块)完成最后一降:降到 1 伏以下。

AI 把这段楼梯的最后一级踩塌了。在 48-54V 下,一个 600kW 级机柜要取约 12,500 安培电流——兆瓦级机柜光铜母排就得用约 200 公斤,电源架还要挤占本该放算力的空间。改用 800V 直流,同一个机柜只需约 750 安:电流少约 16.7 倍,电阻发热低约 220-278 倍。这就是全行业转向 800VDC 的原因(英伟达的参考设计;Google/Meta/微软的 ±400V 变体则复用电动车供应链):整流环节搬出机柜,住进专用的「电源柜」sidecar(单价约 40-50 万美元);中央 UPS 最终被淘汰;端到端电气效率从约 82% 升向约 87.4%——在吉瓦级园区上等于凭空多出约 50MW 电力。终局是固态变压器:一台半导体设备,直接从中压变出 800VDC。

sections.body_en:
PUE — Power Usage Effectiveness — is the single number the industry uses to grade the machine: total facility power divided by IT power. A PUE of 1.6 means that for every watt reaching a computer, another 0.6W is spent on overhead. Where does the overhead go? Cooling takes 60-80% of it; electrical conversion losses (every transformer and UPS stage on the staircase above leaks a little heat) take 15-30%.

The industry average is about 1.6 — and, crucially, that Uptime Institute survey figure excludes hyperscalers. Google and Meta run around 1.1, Microsoft and AWS around 1.15, and Meta's best design hits 1.08. They get there with mechanisms, not magic: free cooling — using outside air or water whenever the weather allows, instead of running energy-hungry compressor chillers; running servers hot, with inlet air above 30°C, which only works because they design their own servers; and obsessive airflow engineering, exploiting the cubic Fan Law — cut airflow 10% and fan energy falls ~27%.

Why an investor should care about a decimal: at a 100MW-IT site, the gap between PUE 1.6 and 1.1 is 50MW of facility load — power that either buys more GPUs or simply does not exist at a grid-constrained site. In the power-scarce world of the next section, PUE stops being an environmental metric and becomes a capacity multiplier. Two caveats keep you honest: PUE says nothing about water (that is WUE, liters per kWh — evaporative cooling trades water for electricity), and it says nothing about losses inside the IT itself, which is exactly what the 800VDC transition attacks.
sections.body_zh:
PUE(电能使用效率)是业界给这台机器打分的那个数:设施总功率除以 IT 功率。PUE 1.6 意味着每有 1 瓦真正送到计算机,还要另花 0.6 瓦在「杂费」上。杂费去了哪?散热占 60-80%;电气转换损耗(上一节那段楼梯上的每一级变压器、每一台 UPS 都会漏一点热)占 15-30%。

行业平均值约 1.6——关键是,Uptime Institute 这个调查数字把超大规模厂商排除在外。Google 和 Meta 跑在 1.1 左右,微软和 AWS 约 1.15,Meta 最好的设计做到 1.08。他们靠的是机制而不是魔法:自然冷却——天气允许时直接用外部空气或水,而不是开耗电的压缩机冷机;把服务器「热着跑」,进风温度超过 30°C——这只有自己设计服务器才敢做;再加上偏执的气流工程,利用三次方的风扇定律——风量降 10%,风扇能耗降约 27%。

为什么投资人要在意小数点后一位?在一个 IT 负载 100MW 的站点,PUE 1.6 和 1.1 之间差着 50MW 的设施负荷——这些电要么拿去多买 GPU,要么在电网卡脖子的站点上根本就不存在。在下一节那个缺电的世界里,PUE 不再是环保指标,而是产能乘数。两条附注让你保持清醒:PUE 完全不反映用水(那是 WUE,升/千瓦时——蒸发冷却本质上是拿水换电);它也不反映 IT 内部的损耗——而后者恰恰是 800VDC 变革要打的靶子。

sections.heading_en: Cooling: The End of the Air Era
sections.body_en:
Everything the power chain delivers, the cooling chain must carry away — the same megawatts, in reverse, as heat. For thirty years air did the job: cold air pushed through the room by CRAH units or whole fan walls, guided into cold aisles, sucked through the servers by their own fans, exhausted into hot aisles, and carried back to heat exchangers. Air is wonderful because it is free and can't leak onto electronics — and terrible because it holds very little heat per liter. As chips passed ~1,000W, air cooling remained possible only in bulky low-density chassis; what actually forced liquid was rack density economics — packing 72 GPUs into one 120kW NVLink rack leaves no room for the airflow required.

So AI hardware switched the medium. In direct-to-chip liquid cooling (DLC), a cold plate — a metal block laced with fine channels — is clamped onto each chip, and coolant flows directly against the silicon. Nvidia's GB200 NVL72, the highest-volume Blackwell rack, is liquid-cooled only: 120kW in a single cabinet, unthinkable with air. The rack's loops connect through quick disconnects — no-drip couplings that let a technician pull a server without draining the system; Nvidia's ramp made even these humble parts a shortage item. The rack's heat then flows to a CDU (coolant distribution unit, often >1MW of heat exchange), which transfers it into the facility's water loop; from there it leaves the building through cooling towers, dry coolers or chillers. A halfway technology, the rear-door heat exchanger (RDHx, a radiator bolted where the rack's back door was, 30-50kW), bridges older air-built halls into the liquid era — xAI's Memphis site famously mixed RDHx with DLC.

For investors, one structural fact: cooling is the second-largest capex system after electrical, and the fastest-evolving — which makes it simultaneously the growth story (CDUs, cold plates, quick disconnects) and the obsolescence risk of the sector.
sections.body_zh:
供电链送进来的每一兆瓦,散热链都得原样搬走——同样的功率,反向流动,以热的形式。过去三十年这活由空气干:CRAH 空调或整面风墙把冷风推进机房,导入冷通道,被服务器自带的风扇吸过机身,吹进热通道,再回到换热器。空气的好处是免费、漏了也不伤电子设备;坏处是每升空气装不了多少热。当单颗芯片功耗越过约 1,000 瓦,风冷仍然可行,但只能靠体积庞大、低密度的机箱;真正逼出液冷的是机柜密度的经济账——把 72 颗 GPU 塞进一个 120kW 的 NVLink 机柜,根本挤不下所需的风量。

于是 AI 硬件换了介质。芯片直接液冷(DLC)把冷板——一块内部刻满细流道的金属块——直接压在每颗芯片上,让冷却液贴着硅片流过。英伟达出货量最大的 Blackwell 机柜 GB200 NVL72 只支持液冷:单柜 120kW,风冷想都不用想。机柜内外的管路靠快接头连接——不滴漏的接插件,让技术员不放空整个系统就能拔出一台服务器;英伟达的产能爬坡让这种不起眼的小零件都成了短缺品。机柜的热接着汇入 CDU(冷量分配单元,换热能力常常超过 1MW),转移进设施水环路,最后经冷却塔、干冷器或冷机离开建筑。还有一种过渡技术——后门换热器(RDHx,把机柜后门换成一面散热器,30-50kW),让为风冷而建的老机房也能挤进液冷时代:xAI 孟菲斯站点就以 RDHx 混搭 DLC 而闻名。

给投资人记一条结构性事实:散热是仅次于电气的第二大资本开支系统,也是演进最快的系统——这让它同时成为这个板块的成长故事(CDU、冷板、快接头)和过时风险所在。

sections.heading_en: Why Power Became the Binding Constraint
sections.heading_zh: 为什么电力成了那道卡脖子的约束
sections.body_en:
For decades, datacenter operators treated electricity like water from a tap: apply to the utility, wait, get connected. AI broke that assumption with sheer arithmetic. The US datacenter buildout is running at +21GW in 2026, heading toward +84GW a year by 2030 — against a US grid whose all-time peak load is about 759GW (set July 2025) and which adds only ~15GW of net-new firm capacity a year. Roughly a terawatt of load requests now sits in US interconnection queues; a grid connection takes ~5 years (7 in Northern Virginia), and on current math grid headroom — supply minus peak demand minus required reserves — turns negative across a growing set of US regions by 2027. The grid is, in SemiAnalysis's phrase, sold out.

What makes the shortage explosive is the economics stacked on top. At ~$10-12M of revenue per MW per year, getting 200MW online six months earlier is worth about a billion dollars — so 'speed is the moat', and any amount of money spent to jump the queue is rational. The queue-jump is called behind-the-meter (BTM) power: instead of waiting for the utility, you generate onsite — gas turbines, reciprocating engines, fuel cells. xAI proved the play by building Colossus in ~122 days on rented, truck-mounted turbines; OpenAI and Oracle followed with a 2.3GW onsite order in Texas. BTM powered under 7% of new US datacenter capacity in 2025; SemiAnalysis expects it to power more than half by 2028 — which is why gas-turbine makers (GE Vernova, Siemens Energy, Mitsubishi) are order-booked into 2028-29.

One last wrinkle completes the picture: AI is not just a big load but a violently twitchy one. Synchronized GPU training swings power ~15x more than a cloud datacenter (Google measured 1.5MW→15MW swings), fast enough to threaten grid stability — pulling batteries, flywheels and capacitor banks into the machine's bill of materials too.
sections.body_zh:
几十年来,数据中心运营商把电当自来水:向电力公司申请,等着,接上。AI 用简单的算术打破了这个假设。美国数据中心建设 2026 年新增 21GW,到 2030 年将冲向每年 84GW——而美国电网的历史峰值负荷约 759GW(2025 年 7 月创下),每年净新增的可靠容量只有约 15GW。如今排在美国并网队列里的负荷请求接近 1 太瓦;拿到一个电网接入要等约 5 年(北弗吉尼亚要 7 年);按当前的账算,电网余量——供给减峰值需求减必要备用——到 2027 年将在越来越多的美国区域转负。用 SemiAnalysis 的说法:电网已经卖空了。

让短缺变得爆炸性的,是叠在上面的经济学。按每 MW 每年约 1,000-1,200 万美元的收入,让 200MW 提前六个月上线价值约 10 亿美元——所以「速度即护城河」,为插队花的任何钱都是理性的。这个插队动作叫表后发电(BTM):不等电力公司,自己在场地内发电——燃气轮机、往复式内燃机、燃料电池。xAI 验证了这个打法:靠租来的卡车装载燃机,约 122 天建成 Colossus;OpenAI 和甲骨文跟进,在得州下了 2.3GW 的自建订单。2025 年 BTM 只为不到 7% 的美国新增数据中心容量供电;SemiAnalysis 预计到 2028 年将超过一半——这也是燃机三巨头(GE Vernova、西门子能源、三菱)订单排到 2028-29 年的原因。

最后一道褶皱补全全图:AI 不只是一个大负荷,还是一个抽搐得厉害的负荷。同步化的 GPU 训练让功率摆动比云数据中心大约 15 倍(Google 实测 1.5MW→15MW),快到足以威胁电网稳定——于是电池、飞轮、电容器组也被拉进了这台机器的物料清单。

images.caption_en: Where the power chain begins: the dedicated electrical substation of Google's datacenter in The Dalles, Oregon, with the facility's cooling towers behind it — a power plant's worth of load with a building wrapped around it.
images.caption_zh: 电力链的起点:Google 俄勒冈州 The Dalles 数据中心的专用变电站,背后是园区的冷却塔——一座电厂级的负荷,外面裹着一层房子。

images.caption_en: The last stop of every watt: rooftop heat-rejection units on a datacenter in Mesa, Arizona. Whatever the power chain delivers, arrays of fans and heat exchangers like these must carry away as heat.
images.caption_zh: 每一瓦电的最后一站:亚利桑那州 Mesa 一座数据中心屋顶的排热机组。供电链送进来多少功率,这样成排的风扇与换热器就得以热的形式搬走多少。

images.caption_en: White space in the air-cooled era: rack rows at CERN's datacenter, cooled by room air. Racks of this generation drew a few kW each; a single GB200 NVL72 AI rack draws 120kW — the jump that ended the air era.
images.caption_zh: 风冷时代的白空间:CERN 数据中心的机柜列,靠房间空气散热。这一代机柜单柜只有几 kW;如今一个 GB200 NVL72 AI 机柜要 120kW——正是这一跳终结了风冷时代。

own_fig.concept_en: The voltage staircase from grid to GPU, and what PUE means at 100MW
own_fig.concept_zh: 从电网到 GPU 的电压楼梯,以及 100MW 尺度下 PUE 的含义
own_fig.spec: Two panels. TOP PANEL 'The power chain (voltage staircase)': a left-to-right flow of 7 boxes drawn as descending steps — [Grid transmission 115-345kV AC] → [Onsite substation] → [Medium voltage 13.8-45kV] → [Transformer + switchgear + UPS (grey space), ~415V AC] → [Overhead busway → rack] → [Rack power shelves → 54V DC busbar] → [VRM at GPU, <1V]. Above the last two steps add a red annotation bracket 'AI breaks this step: 600kW rack @48-54V = ~12,500A vs @800VDC = ~750A (~16.7x less current, ~219-278x less I2R)' and a green arrow showing the 800VDC redesign bypassing the 54V step: [800VDC power-rack sidecar, ~$400-500k] feeding the rack directly; footnote 'end-to-end electrical efficiency ~82% → ~87.4%'. BOTTOM PANEL 'PUE at a 100MW-IT site': two stacked horizontal bars. Bar 1 'Industry average PUE 1.6': IT = 100MW (blue) + cooling ≈ 42MW (orange) + electrical losses & other ≈ 18MW (grey), total 160MW. Bar 2 'Hyperscaler PUE 1.1': IT = 100MW + overhead 10MW, total 110MW. Callout between bars: 'gap = 50MW — enough facility power for ~415 more GB200 NVL72 racks at 120kW'. Overhead split uses the cooling-60-80%/electrical-15-30% midpoints. Data sources: 12,500A vs 750A, 16.7x/219-278x, $400-500k sidecar, 82%→87.4% from SemiAnalysis 'Inside the 800VDC Revolution – Part 1' (newsletter.semianalysis.com/p/inside-the-800vdc-revolution-part); PUE 1.6 vs 1.08-1.15 and cooling 60-80% / electrical 15-30% of overhead from SemiAnalysis 'Datacenter Anatomy Part 2 – Cooling Systems' (semianalysis.com/2025/02/13/datacenter-anatomy-part-2-cooling-systems/); 120kW GB200 NVL72 rack from the same article; voltage-chain stages from SemiAnalysis 'Datacenter Anatomy Part 1: Electrical Systems' (semianalysis.com/2024/10/14/datacenter-anatomy-part-1-electrical/).

why_en: Power and cooling are where AI capex crashes into the physical economy — and where the widest circle of non-semiconductor stocks gets pulled into the trade: turbines (GE Vernova, Siemens Energy), electrical gear (Vertiv, Eaton, Schneider, Delta), liquid cooling, batteries, even utilities. This module gives you the mechanism behind each: why 800VDC is physics rather than fashion (I²R), why PUE is a capacity multiplier in a power-short world, why 'speed is the moat' makes behind-the-meter gas rational at almost any price. It also arms you against the sector's two recurring narrative traps: blaming AI for electric bills, and reading every turbine backlog as a permanent new normal rather than a queue-jump that ends when the grid catches up.
why_zh: 电力与散热是 AI 资本开支撞上实体经济的地方——也是被卷入这场交易的非半导体股票范围最广的地方:燃机(GE Vernova、西门子能源)、电气设备(Vertiv、伊顿、施耐德、台达)、液冷、电池,甚至公用事业。本讲给了你每个环节背后的机制:为什么 800VDC 是物理规律而非潮流(I²R)、为什么在缺电的世界里 PUE 是产能乘数、为什么「速度即护城河」让表后天然气几乎不计代价也划算。它还能帮你识破这个板块反复出现的两个叙事陷阱:把居民电费上涨算到 AI 头上;以及把每一条燃机订单积压都读成永久的新常态——而它可能只是一次插队,电网追上来就结束。
